title: cornus alba var. gouchaultii
intro: Cornus alba var. gouchaultii, also known as Dog's Blood or Red Barked Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its bright red bark and clusters of white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Cornus alba var. gouchaultii have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to support immune function and relieve digestive issues.
